title: Food For Peace & Thought: China-US Agricultural Cooperation - Panel Two
description:
Panel Two:
Musical Performance - Deep River and Battle Cry of Freedom 0:00
Schiller Institute NYC Chorus
Diane Sare, conductor

Introduction - Dennis Speed - Moderator, Northeast Coordinator, Schiller Institute  5:39

Mei Fangquan -Professor at the Agricultural Information Institute, Chief Expert of the UN Global Food Security Committee, China 6:45

Prof. Beverly Silver Chairman of the Dept. of Sociology and Dir. of the Arrighi Center for Global Studies at the Johns Hopkins Univ. 26:20

Wen Tiejun - Executive Dean, Professor, College of Agriculture and Rural Development, Renmin University, China 44:16

Benjamin Deniston - Researcher, 21st Century Science and Technology magazine; co-author, The New Silk Road Becomes the World Land-Bridge 1:07:10

Zhou Yingheng - Professor of Nanjing Agricultural University, China 1:27:36

Dr. Hal B. H. Cooper Jr. - Chairman, Seattle Freight Transport Advisory Board; expert on the Bering Strait railroad tunnel 1:48:46

The Schiller Institute, in collaboration with the China Energy Fund Committee and the Foundation for the Revival of Classical Culture, held an all-day conference in New York City on July 7, 2017, which brought together farm leaders, agricultural scientists, engineers, and political and diplomatic officials from throughout the United States, China, and other nations. The conference attendees included more than 175 farm leaders, agricultural scientists and engineers, prominent speakers from China, diplomats from United Nations missions, New York City area contacts of all kinds, and an impressive delegation from the Midwest farm states, including four from South Dakota, two from Minnesota, and two from Iowa.
